reasoning.issue: The question asks what test the build-up (Cumulative Amount) basket is based on and when the basket starts growing.
reasoning.analysis: The Cumulative Amount is defined as the sum (without duplication) of multiple sources, structured in two tiers: (1) a 'greatest-of' test among three financial metrics (the 'three-test aggregate'), which contributes as a single component, plus (2) several additive sources summed on top. The three-test aggregate (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate) selects the greatest of: (a) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income since the anchor date [6e76ed06_builder_source_cni]; (b) the Available Retained ECF Amount — the portion of Excess Cash Flow not required to be swept to prepay Term Loans under Section 2.10(f) [6e76ed06_builder_source_ecf]; and (c) 140% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period [6e76ed06_builder_source_ebitda_fc]. On top of the greatest-of result, the following additive sources are summed: a starter amount equal to the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period [6e76ed06_builder_source_starter]; 100% of equity proceeds Not Otherwise Applied [6e76ed06_builder_source_other_equity_proceeds]; retained Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales (i.e., proceeds retained after applying the leverage-based sweep tiers) [6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds]; investment returns (dividends, repayments, proceeds from investments) [6e76ed06_builder_source_investment_returns]; and amounts from debt-to-equity conversions [6e76ed06_builder_source_other_debt_conversion]. The basket is reduced by prior usage for RP, RDP, and investment purposes (reduced_by_rp_usage, reduced_by_rdp_usage, reduced_by_investment_usage = true). Start date: the basket commences accumulation on the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs, per the start_date_language attribute on the builder_basket entity and confirmed by the growth_start_anchor = 'closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start' on all source norms.
reasoning.interactions: The three-test aggregate (6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate) feeds into the builder_usage_permission (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) via a greatest_of aggregation (child_index 0). The starter, equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt conversion sources each contribute additively (child_index 1, 5, 6, 7, 8 respectively) via sum aggregation. Retained asset sale proceeds flow in from asset_sale_event proceeds after sweep-tier exemptions apply. The Cumulative Amount is shared with the RDP builder basket (6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission / Section 6.09(a)(A)), subject to intercreditor restrictions.
reasoning.conclusion: The Cumulative Amount basket is based on a 'greatest of' test among 50% of cumulative CNI, retained ECF, and 140% of LTM EBITDA, plus a separate starter amount and several additive sources; it begins accumulating from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

stage2.answer: The 'Cumulative Amount' (build-up basket) is structured as follows: (1) a core 'greatest of' test selecting among (a) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income, (b) the Available Retained ECF Amount (Excess Cash Flow not swept to repay Term Loans under Section 2.10(f)), and (c) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA; plus (2) additive sources including a starter of the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, 100% of equity proceeds Not Otherwise Applied, retained asset sale proceeds (after leverage-based sweep), investment returns, and debt-to-equity conversion amounts [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]. The basket is reduced dollar-for-dollar by prior usage for restricted payments, restricted debt payments, and investments. The basket begins growing on the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32; builder_basket entity, start_date_language]. The Cumulative Amount is also shared with the RDP builder basket under Section 6.09(a)(A), subject to applicable intercreditor restrictions [Section 6.09(a)(A), p.199].

questions.gold_answer: The Cumulative Amount is based on the greatest of three tests: (1) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income (which amount shall not be less than zero for any fiscal quarter), (2) Excess Cash Flow not required to be applied to prepay Term Loans or any other debt (such amount cannot be less than $0 for any fiscal year), (3) cumulative Consolidated EBITDA minus 140% of cumulative Consolidated Fixed Charges. All tests start growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower is permitted under the restricted payments covenant to dividend (distribute) equity interests it holds in Unrestricted Subsidiaries to its own equityholders or up the corporate chain.
reasoning.analysis: The primary norm at issue is norm_id 6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission, extracted from the unsub_distribution_basket at Section 6.06(p) (p.198). That provision expressly permits 'the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of... an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof.' The basket is categorical (is_categorical: true, capacity_category: 'categorical') and carries no dollar cap, no leverage test, and no condition tree. The v3 attributes confirm: covers_equity_interests: true, covers_proceeds: true, partial_applicability: false, and requires_valid_designation: false. Accordingly, the Borrower may distribute equity of an Unrestricted Subsidiary (or the proceeds of such equity) by dividend without consuming any other basket or satisfying any financial ratio. The basket also rebuilds the builder basket (rebuilds_builder_basket: true) and distributions can cascade (distributions_can_cascade: true), meaning the distributed equity interests can themselves be further distributed. The supplementary general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission, §6.06(j)) is not necessary to support this permission; it is a separate, capped basket for cash dividends generally. The J.Crew blocker (6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition, §Definition of Unrestricted Subsidiary, p.89) constrains designation of a Restricted Subsidiary as an Unrestricted Subsidiary if it holds Material IP, but it does not restrict the distribution of equity in an already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiary—so it does not limit the §6.06(p) permission.
reasoning.interactions: The §6.06(p) permission is standalone and categorical; it does not draw from or interact with the builder basket or general RP basket capacity. The J.Crew blocker operates at the designation stage and does not defeat the distribution permission post-designation. No defeaters are listed against 6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes. Section 6.06(p) provides a categorical, uncapped permission for the Borrower to distribute by dividend the equity interests (or proceeds thereof) of any Unrestricted Subsidiary, with no dollar limit, leverage test, or other condition.

stage2.answer: Yes. Section 6.06(p) (p.198) expressly and categorically permits the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of an Unrestricted Subsidiary (or the proceeds thereof). This basket is uncapped, requires no financial ratio compliance, and does not require a valid designation at the time of distribution. The J.Crew blocker at the Definition of Unrestricted Subsidiary (p.89) applies only to the designation of a Restricted Subsidiary as an Unrestricted Subsidiary and does not restrict distributions of equity in an already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiary.

reasoning.issue: The question asks whether any investment, restricted debt payment (RDP), or other baskets can be reallocated and used to make restricted payments or dividends under the credit agreement.
reasoning.analysis:
Four key reallocation pathways exist:

1. GENERAL RDP BASKET → RP (cross-covenant fungibility): The general RDP basket under Section 6.09(a)(I) has a cap of the greater of $130M and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA, with action_scope='reallocable'. The general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) has an identical cap (greater of $130M / 100% EBITDA) with capacity_composition='fungible' and action_scope='reallocable'. These two baskets share the same dollar pool — capacity used under one reduces availability under the other. A borrower may use this shared pool either to prepay subordinated debt or to make dividends/RPs.

2. BUILDER BASKET (Cumulative Amount) → Both RP and RDP: The builder basket (Cumulative Amount, defined at p.32) is a single general_purpose pool used for RPs (dividends/equity repurchases), RDPs (subordinated debt prepayments), AND investments. The builder_rdp_basket (Section 6.09(a)(A)) expressly draws on the Cumulative Amount (shares_with_rp_builder=true), confirming the Cumulative Amount is shared across RP and RDP covenants. Usage under any of these three categories reduces the same pool (reduced_by_rp_usage=true, reduced_by_rdp_usage=true, reduced_by_investment_usage=true). The builder basket starts at the greater of $130M / 100% EBITDA and grows via 50% CNI, retained ECF, 140% EBITDA fixed charge coverage formula, 100% equity proceeds, investment returns, retained asset sale proceeds, and debt conversion proceeds.

3. INVESTMENT BASKET → Cumulative Amount (Section 6.03(x)): investment_pathway_2 (Section 6.03(x)) permits investments up to the Cumulative Amount. Because the Cumulative Amount is shared, investment usage draws from the same pool as RP and RDP usage — meaning the Cumulative Amount is the cross-covenant fungible link between the investment covenant and the RP/RDP covenants.

4. INVESTMENT BASKET → RDP Basket (Section 6.03(y)): investment_pathway_3 (Section 6.03(y)) has a cap equal to the greater of $130M/100% EBITDA PLUS all amounts available under Section 6.09(a) (the RDP basket). This means RDP capacity is explicitly included in the investment basket formula, creating a cross-covenant link where unused RDP capacity feeds investment capacity (though the direction here is RDP → investments, not directly RDP → RP dividends).
reasoning.interactions: The general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission, §6.06(j)) and general RDP basket (6e76ed06_general_rdp_basket_permission, §6.09(a)(I)) share a fungible $130M/100% EBITDA pool — usage under either reduces the other. The builder basket (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) is a single Cumulative Amount pool shared across RP, RDP, and investment covenants (reduced_by_rp_usage, reduced_by_rdp_usage, reduced_by_investment_usage all true). The builder RDP basket (6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission) draws on the same Cumulative Amount as the builder RP basket (shares_with_rp_builder=true). Investment pathway §6.03(x) also draws from the Cumulative Amount, creating three-way fungibility. No defeaters override these cross-covenant links.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — there are two primary reallocation mechanisms: (1) the general RP and general RDP baskets share a single fungible pool (greater of $130M/100% EBITDA), so capacity not used for RDPs is available for dividends and vice versa; and (2) the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) is a single pool shared across the RP, RDP, and investment covenants, so returns from investments and other builder sources can be used for restricted payments or subordinated debt prepayments interchangeably.

stage2.answer: Yes, there are two principal cross-covenant reallocation mechanisms. First, the general RP basket [Section 6.06(j), p.196] and the general RDP basket [Section 6.09(a)(I), p.200] share a single fungible pool capped at the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA — capacity not consumed by subordinated debt prepayments is available for dividends/restricted payments and vice versa. Second, the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32] is a single general-purpose pool that feeds the RP covenant (dividends and equity repurchases under §6.06), the RDP covenant (subordinated debt prepayments under §6.09(a)(A)), and the investment covenant (§6.03(x)), with usage under any of the three categories reducing the shared pool; builder sources include 50% of cumulative CNI, retained ECF, 140% EBITDA fixed-charge formula, 100% equity proceeds, investment returns, and retained asset-sale proceeds. Additionally, the investment basket under Section 6.03(y) incorporates the full amount available under Section 6.09(a) (the RDP basket) as an additive component of its own cap, creating a further cross-covenant link between RDP and investment capacity.

reasoning.issue: Whether asset sale proceeds can be used to fund dividend payments under the credit agreement's restricted payments covenant.
reasoning.analysis: The key mechanism is the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) definition. Norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds establishes that net cash proceeds from asset sales that are retained after the mandatory prepayment sweep (i.e., not required to be applied to prepay term loans under Section 2.10(c)) feed into the Cumulative Amount as one of its additive sources (child_index 6 of the builder basket). The proceeds_flow confirms this: asset sale proceeds flow into the builder basket clause as 'retained_after_sweep' — specifically, proceeds that survive the leverage-based sweep tier exemptions or fall below de minimis thresholds. Norm 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission confirms that the Cumulative Amount, once built, expressly permits make_dividend_payment as a scoped action. The builder basket's v3 attributes confirm has_asset_proceeds_source: true, capacity_category: general_purpose, and that the basket is reduced by RP usage. The sweep mechanics are relevant: at First Lien Leverage Ratio >5.75x, 100% of net cash proceeds must prepay term loans (sweep_tier_100pct); at >5.50x but ≤5.75x, 50% must sweep; at ≤5.50x, 0% sweeps and all proceeds are retainable. Only the portion not swept (i.e., 'retained' proceeds) enters the Cumulative Amount and can be used for dividends. Additionally, the general_rp_basket_permission (Section 6.06(j)) and the ratio basket (Section 6.06(o)) provide independent dividend capacity not dependent on asset sale proceeds.
reasoning.interactions: The asset sale proceeds source (6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds) contributes additively (child_index 6) to the builder usage permission (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) via the sum aggregation function. The sweep tiers (6e76ed06_sweep_tier_100pct, _50pct, _0pct) gate how much of the proceeds survive to enter the builder basket — at ≤5.50x leverage, the full amount is retained and available; at higher leverage ratios, only the unswept portion is retained. The builder basket is reduced by subsequent RP usage (reduced_by_rp_usage: true), so dividends paid from the Cumulative Amount draw it down.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes, asset sale proceeds can be used to make dividends, but only to the extent such proceeds are 'retained' after the mandatory leverage-based sweep — the retained portion enters the Cumulative Amount builder basket, which expressly permits dividend payments.

stage2.answer: Yes, asset sale net cash proceeds can be used to fund dividends, but only the portion that is retained after the mandatory prepayment sweep under Section 2.10(c). The retained proceeds flow into the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) as an additive source [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32], and the Cumulative Amount expressly permits dividend payments [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]. The amount available depends on the leverage-based sweep tiers: if First Lien Leverage Ratio >5.75x, 100% of proceeds must prepay term loans (nothing retained); if >5.50x but ≤5.75x, 50% must sweep; if ≤5.50x, 0% sweeps and all proceeds are retainable [Section 2.10(c) / Definition of Applicable Net Cash Proceeds Percentage, clauses (a)–(c), pp.5–6]. Any proceeds that survive the sweep (or fall below de minimis thresholds) add to the Cumulative Amount, which is then drawn down by dividend payments made thereunder.

Also includes asset sale proceeds not swept when First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 5.75x or less (50% of proceeds) or 5.50x or less (100% of proceeds). Also includes Net Cash Proceeds from non-collateral assets, ordinary course asset sales, asset sales from non-ratio baskets, casualty events, and proceeds from collateral assets below de minimis thresholds of $20M/15% EBITDA individual and $40M/30% EBITDA annual.

reasoning.issue: The question asks for the total quantifiable dividend capacity across all baskets in the restricted payments covenant, requiring identification and summation of general_purpose baskets, and separate listing of restricted_purpose, unlimited_conditional, and categorical baskets.
reasoning.analysis:
Step 1 — Classify baskets by capacity_category:

(A) GENERAL_PURPOSE baskets (sum independently unless shared pool):
1. General RP Basket [6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission, §6.06(j)]: greater of $130M and 100% Consolidated EBITDA. capacity_composition = 'fungible' — this basket shares a pool with the General RDP Basket [6e76ed06_general_rdp_basket_permission, §6.09(a)(I)], which has the identical cap (greater of $130M / 100% EBITDA). However, no explicit 'shares_capacity_pool' link is present in the data between these two. The general RDP basket is a separate covenant (§6.09 RDP), not §6.06 RP. They are independent pools for dividend purposes. Quantifiable floor: $130M each.

2. Builder Basket [6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission, §Definition of Cumulative Amount]: general_purpose, capacity_composition = computed_from_sources, aggregation = greatest_of across three tests + additive sources. The three-test aggregate [6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate] takes the greatest of: (a) 50% cumulative CNI [6e76ed06_builder_source_cni]; (b) Available Retained ECF Amount [6e76ed06_builder_source_ecf] (variable, floor $0/year); (c) 140% of Consolidated EBITDA [6e76ed06_builder_source_ebitda_fc]. Then, the Cumulative Amount = that greatest-of result PLUS: starter amount (greater of $130M / 100% EBITDA) [6e76ed06_builder_source_starter] + 100% equity proceeds [6e76ed06_builder_source_other_equity_proceeds] + retained asset sale proceeds + investment returns + debt conversion proceeds. The only quantifiable fixed floor is the starter: $130M. The CNI, ECF, EBITDA-FC, equity proceeds, and other additive sources are variable (deal-specific). Reduced by prior RP/RDP/investment usage.

(B) UNLIMITED_CONDITIONAL baskets (not quantifiable — unlimited if ratio met):
- Ratio RP Basket [6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission, §6.06(o)]: unlimited dividends if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x pro forma OR no-worse test met.

(C) RESTRICTED_PURPOSE baskets (listed separately; not addable to general total):
- Management Equity Basket [6e76ed06_management_equity_basket_permission, §6.06(b)]: greater of $20M / 15% Consolidated EBITDA per annum (equity repurchases only, with carryforward).
- Tax Distribution Basket [§6.06(c)]: categorical, standalone taxpayer limit — not dollar-quantifiable.
- Holdco Overhead Basket [§6.06(k)]: categorical — not dollar-quantifiable.
- Equity Award Basket [§6.06(d)]: restricted_purpose/unlimited_on_condition — cashless exercise/tax withholding only.

(D) CATEGORICAL baskets:
- Unrestricted Subsidiary Distribution Basket [§6.06(p)]: categorical — distributes unsub equity/assets at FMV, no dollar cap.

Step 2 — Sum general_purpose baskets for dividends specifically:
- General RP Basket: $130M fixed floor (grower: 100% EBITDA)
- Builder Basket fixed floor: $130M starter (grower components variable)
- These are independent pools (no shares_capacity_pool link).
- Fixed floor total (general_purpose, dividend-applicable): $130M (General RP) + $130M (Builder starter) = $260M minimum, growing with EBITDA and other accretion.

Note: The General RDP Basket ($130M / 100% EBITDA) applies to subordinated debt payments, not dividends directly — it is a separate covenant basket. It is noted but excluded from the dividend-specific general_purpose sum.
reasoning.interactions: The Builder Basket [6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission] is reduced by prior RP, RDP, and investment usage (reduced_by_rp_usage, reduced_by_rdp_usage, reduced_by_investment_usage = true), so usage under §6.06(j) or §6.03 investments draws down the Cumulative Amount. The General RP Basket [6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission] has capacity_composition = 'fungible' but no shares_capacity_pool link to the builder basket, so they are independent pools. The Ratio RP Basket [6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission] is conditioned on First Lien Leverage ≤ 5.75x (or no-worse), making it unlimited but contingent — it does not add to the fixed quantifiable total.
reasoning.conclusion: Quantifiable dividend capacity has a fixed-dollar floor of $260M (greater of $130M/100% EBITDA under §6.06(j) + greater of $130M/100% EBITDA as the builder basket starter), each growing with Consolidated EBITDA and, for the builder, with cumulative CNI, ECF, EBITDA-FC, and equity proceeds accretion. Additionally, unlimited capacity is available under the ratio basket (§6.06(o)) if First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤ 5.75x pro forma or no-worse.

stage2.answer: The quantifiable dividend capacity consists of two independent general_purpose pools with a combined fixed-dollar floor of $260M: (1) the General RP Basket [Section 6.06(j), p.196] — greater of $130M and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA LTM, available for dividends to Holdings and its equity holders; and (2) the Builder Basket / Cumulative Amount [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32] — which starts at the greater of $130M and 100% Consolidated EBITDA, then grows cumulatively by the greatest of 50% of Consolidated Net Income, the Available Retained ECF Amount, or 140% of Consolidated EBITDA (each measured since the first day of the fiscal quarter of the Closing Date), plus 100% of equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt-to-equity conversions, reduced by prior RP/RDP/investment usage. Separately, unlimited dividends are available under the Ratio RP Basket [Section 6.06(o), p.198] if, on a pro forma basis, First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤ 5.75x or no worse than the pre-transaction ratio. Restricted-purpose baskets include the Management Equity Basket [Section 6.06(b), p.195] (greater of $20M/15% EBITDA per annum, for employee equity repurchases only, with carryforward), plus categorical baskets for tax distributions [§6.06(c)], holdco overhead [§6.06(k)], equity award cashless exercise [§6.06(d)], and unrestricted subsidiary distributions [§6.06(p)] — none of which are addable to the general dividend total.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower can dividend a $200m asset/business division with negative EBITDA to shareholders when the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.0x, focusing on which RP baskets are available.
reasoning.analysis:
The primary basket to analyze is the unlimited ratio basket under Section 6.06(o) (norm: 6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission). This basket permits unlimited dividends — including dividends of a business_division (explicitly in scope) — subject to an 'or' condition: either (a) pro forma First Lien Net Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x, or (b) the 'no-worse' test: pro forma First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is no greater than the pre-transaction First Lien Net Leverage Ratio.

Condition (a): The absolute threshold is 5.75x. At 6.0x current leverage, condition (a) fails on its face.

Condition (b) — the no-worse test: The pro forma First Lien Net Leverage Ratio must be no greater than the ratio 'determined without giving effect to the Subject Transactions on a Pro Forma Basis' for the most recently ended Test Period. The pre-transaction ratio is 6.0x. The critical question is whether the pro forma ratio after dividending the division would be at or below 6.0x. Because the division has NEGATIVE EBITDA, removing it from the consolidated group INCREASES consolidated EBITDA (the denominator of the leverage ratio). With no assumption of debt retirement (this is a dividend, not a sale generating proceeds to repay debt), the numerator (First Lien Net Debt) stays the same. Therefore, dividing out a negative-EBITDA division directionally IMPROVES (reduces) the leverage ratio on a pro forma basis. If the pro forma ratio after the transaction is ≤ 6.0x (the pre-transaction level), the no-worse test is satisfied. Given that removing negative EBITDA improves the ratio, the pro forma ratio should be ≤ 6.0x, meaning the no-worse test is passed.

Scope check: The norm explicitly binds the borrower_party and scopes the action to make_dividend_payment and the object to business_division — this transaction fits squarely.

General basket (6.06(j), norm 6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission): Provides $130m / 100% Consolidated EBITDA capacity. A $200m asset dividend would consume this basket entirely and likely exceed it; moreover, this basket covers cash and equity_interest but partial_applicability is false, so it may not straightforwardly apply to a non-cash business division dividend. This basket is secondary to the ratio basket analysis.

Builder basket (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission): General purpose, but its current accumulated balance is unknown and would depend on post-closing EBITDA, CNI, ECF, etc. It cannot be sized on the given facts.

Conclusion: The ratio basket's no-worse test is the decisive pathway at 6.0x leverage.
reasoning.interactions: The ratio basket (6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission) has an 'or' condition tree — either absolute ≤5.75x or no-worse test. The no-worse prong is the operative one at 6.0x. The general basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) provides independent capacity but is dollar-capped and may be insufficient for a $200m non-cash dividend. No defeaters are present in the data. The asset_sale_sweep/sweep_tier entities apply to asset sales generating Net Cash Proceeds — a dividend of a division does not generate cash proceeds and those sweep mechanics are not triggered.
reasoning.conclusion: The Borrower can dividend the negative-EBITDA business division under the unlimited ratio basket in Section 6.06(o), provided the no-worse test is satisfied — i.e., the pro forma First Lien Net Leverage Ratio after the transaction is ≤ 6.0x (the pre-transaction level). Because removing a negative-EBITDA division increases consolidated EBITDA and thereby reduces the leverage ratio, the no-worse test is directionally satisfied, making the dividend permissible at a 6.0x starting leverage ratio.

stage2.answer: Yes, the Borrower can dividend the $200m negative-EBITDA business division to shareholders at a 6.0x First Lien Net Leverage Ratio, relying on the unlimited ratio basket under Section 6.06(o) [p. 198] via the 'no-worse' test. Although the absolute 5.75x threshold (condition (x)) is not met at 6.0x, the basket alternatively permits unlimited dividends if the pro forma First Lien Leverage Ratio after the transaction is no greater than the pre-transaction ratio of 6.0x (condition (y)). Because the division carries negative EBITDA, removing it from the consolidated group increases consolidated EBITDA and thus reduces (improves) the leverage ratio on a pro forma basis — meaning the post-transaction ratio will be below 6.0x, satisfying the no-worse test. The norm expressly scopes the object class to include 'business_division' and the action to 'make_dividend_payment,' so this transaction falls squarely within the basket's coverage. The general basket under Section 6.06(j) [p. 196] provides an additional independent dollar capacity of the greater of $130m / 100% Consolidated EBITDA, though it is unlikely to be sufficient on a standalone basis for a $200m non-cash dividend.
